summary: Indeterminate is never silent: a predicate this engine has no judge for fails admissibility (the `dependency-exists` precedent), and `evaluate` stops dropping an undecided clause into a green pass

edit.description: `evaluate` (214-219) collapses `Outcome::Indeterminate` into the empty violation vec — a clause the engine never decided reads as green, the silent degrade `intent.md` invariant 6 forbids. The live instance is the judge-less arm (492-509): `Count`/`Unique`/`Membership`/`Degree`/`Kind` are admissible in a per-artifact `expect` contract today and silently hold there, because this engine judges one kind's population against `all`, never a named requirement's satisfier set. Send them to the admissibility fence exactly as `DependencyExists` already goes (`inadmissibilities` 110-140; its arm's own comment at 125-129 states the rule: "A hand-authored clause must therefore fail admissibility, not degrade to a working no-op"). **The fence is facet-split, never a longer shared list** — `inadmissibilities` is `pub(crate)` precisely so `roster::admissibility` reuses its vacuity rules for a *requirement's* clauses (src/roster.rs:173-176, 219), and there all five DO have a live judge (`crate::roster`/`crate::graph`), so rejecting them in that facet would break the half that works. After the fence the only `Indeterminate` a conformance run can reach is `FormatPlacesEdges`'s absent-fact arm (460-466); `contract.md`, "selection" settles it — a member outside the narrowing is a finding, never a silent exclusion. The `Outcome` doc (220-232), the two comment blocks naming REQUIREMENT-CLAUSES-RECUT as the future that lifts the floor (492-504, 943-947), and the `dependency-exists` doc at 253 all state the retired floor and move with it.

The shared-fence reuse point — the doc at 173-176 ("reusing `crate::engine::inadmissibilities`, the same vacuous-clause rules … so a requirement's demands and a kind's clauses never carry two definitions of `vacuous`") and the call at 219. The facet split lands here as the side that keeps admitting the five: one definition of vacuity still, plus one declared fact about which facet carries a judge — never a second copy of the rules drifting beside the first (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home").

The fail-loud suite drives the real binary (its header: "neither spelling can half-gate into a silent green"). A per-artifact contract carrying one of the five now fails loud at admissibility naming the predicate, rather than passing a check it never ran.

tests.asserts: each of count/unique/membership/degree/kind is inadmissible as a per-artifact clause, and no admissible predicate reaches `Outcome::Indeterminate` at conformance except `format-places-edges`'s absent-fact arm
a requirement's own clauses still admit all five — the facet that carries a judge is unchanged
a contract naming a judge-less predicate fails loud at admissibility, never a green check

acceptance: No clause the engine cannot decide over its selection reads as a green pass: it is an admissibility error or a finding (contract.md, "well-formedness"; intent.md invariant 6 — no path silently degrades over an unresolved input), and a requirement's clauses keep every predicate `roster`/`graph` judges. `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 inbox ruling, entry 1 — landed first so the gap cannot recur silently. The rule pre-exists in code as `dependency-exists`'s fence; the five never joined it. Trap named in the entry: `inadmissibilities` is shared with the requirement facet where the five ARE judged, so a naive push onto the list breaks roster. `extract.rs`'s edge_placements doc is not here — UNFILLED-EDGE-FIELD-NO-EDGE owns that construction site. scoped at d572827.

summary: An unfilled edge field is no edge: emit's refusal retreats to a filled-yet-unresolvable reference, and the engine's placement view ranges over the value's filled edges only

edit.description: `edgeTargetFacts` throws when an edge field's leaf is absent or empty (186-193, the `names no target` arm) — the floor that made a declared edge field mandatory at every instance. Retreat it: an unfilled leaf is no edge, so it contributes no `EdgeTargetFacts` entry and no placement obligation. The two sibling refusals stand (target names no composed member, 194-200; target owns no projection, 201-206) — those are the filled-yet-unresolvable reference `pipeline.md` licenses. `placedEdges` (344-350) builds its obligation from `value.edgeFields`, the kind's whole declared set; range it over the fields this value fills, so the default no-`render` view cannot claim placement of an absent edge. The function's own `# Throws` doc (172-178) states the retired floor and moves with it.

`ResolvedEmbeddedMemberValue.targets`'s doc (356-364) says each *declared* edge field's facts, with an empty map only for a kind declaring none; under 0026 an unfilled field carries no entry either. `EdgeField` (43-47) gains no optionality flag — the kind's own field schema (the SDK type parameter, `cite: Member` vs `cite?: Member`) is optionality's one home, per 0026's Rejected list.

`emit refuses an edge field whose leaf names no target at all` (260-262) pins the retired floor — invert it: an unfilled leaf emits, with no target facts and no placement claim. The two sibling refusal tests (237, 243) and the resolving case (264) stand unchanged.
The `nested_member` placement rows (1097-1200): an edge field the value never filled records no placement obligation, so `placed_edges` cannot read as an omission.

`embedded_member_features` (1481-1517) builds `edge_placements` from `edge_fields_by_kind`'s whole declared set crossed with the row's `placed_edges`, so an unfilled edge field reads `false` — a `format-places-edges` false positive (`intent.md` invariant 2) the moment emit's throw retreats and such a value can emit at all. Range the map over the edge fields the row's own `leaves` fills; the `None` placed_edges case (a layout-hosted value, no format to indict) is unchanged.

The `format-places-edges` clause tests (added 13c58ed): a value leaving a declared edge field unfilled is not an omission — the clause indicts only a format that drops an edge the value actually carries.

tests.asserts: an embedded value whose declared edge field is unfilled emits — no throw, and no target facts for that field
an unfilled edge field is absent from the value's `placed_edges`, while a filled one still records
format-places-edges reports no omission for an edge field the value never filled, and still indicts a dropped filled edge

acceptance: emit refuses no unfilled edge field — refusal reaches only a reference filled yet unresolvable (pipeline.md, "Emit", the Refusing bullet) — and `format-places-edges` indicts no format for an edge the value never carried; `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0026 (fd52717). Its second consequence — the schema's optionality reaching the declared fact row — reads as: the row already carries it (`NestedMemberRow.leaves` names the filled fields), so no-edge-when-absent is decidable off existing row data; required-vs-optional never reaches the engine. Re-gated behind INDETERMINATE-NEVER-SILENT (07-16 ruling): it settles how `format-places-edges`' absent fact reads, and both touch contract_template.rs. scoped at ca4e866, re-gated at d572827.

summary: Locus goes ternary: a nested file child owns a file whose path composes from its host's unit and the host template's pattern, and governs no glob of its own

edit.description: `Locus` (49-59) is binary — `{kind:"at", root, glob}` or `{kind:"embedded"}` — and its doc (50-56) states that binary as the whole story. Grow the third spelling: a nested-file child owns a unit whose path composes from its host's unit plus the host template's `path` pattern (`Template`, 102-118, already carries the pattern), so the child kind declares no `root`/`glob` and cannot collide with its host's governs glob.

`projectionPath` (114-140) refuses every non-`at` locus (129-133, the `is embedded` arm) — the floor that makes a standalone projection an `at`-locus privilege. A nested-file child projects at its host's unit joined with the template's pattern; the `# Throws` doc (123-127) states the retired floor and moves with it. `joinSlash` (110-112) is the existing path composer — extend it, never a second joiner (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home").

The kind fact row's locus columns: `governs_root`/`governs_glob` are unconditional today, and a nested-file kind fills neither. The row carries the third spelling instead of a root+glob pair the kind does not have.
Regenerated seam binding for the grown locus columns (ts-rs output — regenerate, never hand-edit).

`Governs` (29-37) is a required root+glob pair every `CustomKind` carries (`CustomKind::new`, 587-600, takes it by value); `from_kind_fact_row` (644-651) rebuilds it from the row's two columns. A nested-file kind governs no glob, so the locus must admit the host-composed spelling rather than a fabricated root+glob.

`member_projection_path` (578-605) derives a member's projection off `governs_glob`/`governs_root` alone, and the doc (569-577) is the SDK-side twin PROJECTION-PATH-SEAM-GATE (e76ec85) holds in agreement — both sides gain the nested-file case together or the gate fires. Line 1075-1077 joins root+glob the same way. Rider (open-questions): the doc at 570 calls the live SDK `projectionPath` "retired" — reconcile it while here.

The `supportingDoc` fixture (459-465) spells the locus 0027's first Rejected bullet forbids: a self-governing `{at, .claude/skills, *.md}` glob overlapping `skill`'s own `*/SKILL.md`. Respell it as a nested-file child of its `guide` host (467-476); the template-fact assertions (478-487) stand.

The file-child template round-trip (225-245) proves the declared fact only. Extend it onto the locus: the child's projection path derives from the host's unit plus the pattern, on both the SDK and engine sides.
The two-homes agreement gate (e76ec85) ranges over the loci that project; the nested-file spelling enters it, so the SDK and engine derivations cannot diverge on a child's composed path.

schemaDelta: `KindFactRow`'s locus columns grow the third spelling: `governs_root`/`governs_glob` are absent for a nested-file kind, whose path composes from its host's unit and the host template's pattern. No built-in declares the locus until SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS, so `src/builtin_lock.toml` stays byte-identical and the frozen compare does not move.

tests.asserts: a nested file child's projection path composes from its host's unit and the host template's pattern, and the child kind carries no governs glob
the SDK and engine derivations agree on a nested file child's composed path
a host's file child emits at its composed path — the child kind declaring no glob of its own

acceptance: A nested file member owns a file whose path composes from its host's unit and the host template's path pattern, the child kind governing no glob (representation.md, "locus"); two kinds still never share a governs glob. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 1 — the composition surface. TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT (794678f) landed the declared fact; the locus under it was unspellable, which is what forked. Serialized behind UNFILLED-EDGE-FIELD-NO-EDGE (shared kind.ts, emit.ts, emit.test.ts). Discharges the drift.rs:570 "retired projectionPath" rider. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: Discovery classifies an adopted harness's matching files as its host's children through the host template's pattern — the child kind governing no glob to scan for

edit.description: `discover_kind_units` (145-167) is `governs`-keyed — it scans `<harness>/<governs.root>` for `governs.glob` matches — and `discover_kind_files` (114-131) wraps it. A nested-file kind has no `governs` to key on, so nothing discovers its members: its units are found under each *host* unit at the host template's pattern. Extend the one scanner (its doc names it as serving every kind's locus) rather than adding a second walk beside it (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home"); the ignore-honoring `discoverable_paths` set (250-252) governs the child walk exactly as it governs a host's.
The per-kind discovery wiring that feeds `by_kind`: a nested-file kind's members reach it through their host's units, not a root+glob scan, so the host's discovery must run before its children's.

Drift re-runs the same `governs`-keyed scan against a live harness (`import::discover_kind_units` is `pub(crate)` for exactly that) — the nested-file case moves with it, or a discovered child is an ownerless projection to the reaper.
Discovery coverage for the file child: an adopted harness's file matching a host template's pattern classifies as that host's child, of the template's kind.

tests.asserts: a file under a host's unit matching the host template's path pattern is discovered as that host's nested file child, of the template's declared kind
a file under a host's unit that the pattern does not match is discovered as no member — unmodeled, never mis-classified

acceptance: Discovery classifies an adopted harness's matching files as their host's children through the host template's pattern (representation.md, "locus"; the pattern is the host's declared fact) — with no glob of the child kind's own scanned for. `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 2 — discovery off the template pattern. The declared-fact-only bound TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT (794678f) stated in its own body ("Nothing resolves a file child's discovery off the pattern") and in `Template`'s doc (src/kind.rs:576-579) retires here; the doc moves with the behavior. Serialized behind NESTED-FILE-LOCUS: the locus is what a discovered child is classified as. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: The built-in adoption: `skill` templates its bundled reference documents at the directory's markdown, and `supporting-doc` ships as their prose-only kind

edit.description: `skill` (142-158) declares no template. Give it its bundled reference documents as one file-child layer — `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16). New `supporting-doc` kind beside it: fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename, nested-file locus. Its default contract ships **empty** per the standing bar ("Default contracts") — the exported clause array exists so adoption is an import; its one clause is SUPPORTING-DOC-REACH-CLAUSE's to add once an incidence clause outside a requirement is admissible, never this entry's to invent. The kind ships without joining the surface enumeration, the `requirement` precedent.

The engine-side reader mirror of the shipped kinds (`CustomKind::new` calls at 70/91/117/148/173/209/236, and `definitions` at 291): `skill`'s row grows its template, and `supporting-doc` enters as the child kind a discovered document is read as.

The embedded built-in lock re-derives once `skill`'s row carries a template and `supporting-doc` has a row — the first built-in to declare either (TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT left every built-in row's `templates` column absent, so this file was byte-identical there and is not here).
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the two grown rows.
`skill`'s declaration row carries its reference-document template, and `supporting-doc`'s row carries its nested-file locus and empty field schema.

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `skill`'s kind row grows its `templates` column (child kind `supporting-doc`, the markdown path pattern), and `supporting-doc` enters as a new kind row at the nested-file locus.

tests.asserts: `skill` declares one file-child layer of `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, and `supporting-doc` is fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically from the SDK with the two grown rows
a real skill directory's markdown document is discovered as that skill's `supporting-doc` child

acceptance: A skill's bundled reference documents are nested file members of the same skill, typed `supporting-doc` (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"), their path the skill template's declared fact — nesting-is-model-containment / locus-is-serialization stops being an unbacked claim. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 3 — the built-in adoption; 0027 says it re-enters buildable, and it does, minus the clause. The template claims the honest markdown subset: supporting files of other types stay unmodeled and named as such (0027's second Rejected bullet), the `settings.json` partial-governance posture. Discharges the `supportingDocs()` factory asymmetry in open-questions. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: The fixture-harness wiring builder belongs in `tests/common`, not copy-pasted per suite: three copies of tmpdir → `.temper` → `harness.ts` → `vendor_sdk` collapse into one home

edit.description: The declared one home for test scaffolding — its own header (1-4) names "the SDK vendoring used by tests that drive a real `node` subprocess" as the copy-per-suite it exists to end. It carries every ingredient (`tmpdir` 31, `scaffold` 44, `vendor_sdk` 96) and not the builder that composes them into a fixture harness, which is exactly the surface the three copies below each re-derive. Add that builder: a label plus the fixture program text, returning `(harness, into)`. `tests/install.rs`'s eleven `vendor_sdk` calls are a different job — the install verb scaffolds its own `harness.ts` under test, rather than wiring an authored fixture program — and are out of scope.

`wire_sdk_harness_program` (1220-1230) is the original, and the surface the other two copies' doc comments cite by name as the pattern they follow; `wire_sdk_harness` (1214-1216) binds it to this suite's `HARNESS_PROGRAM`. Retire the local body onto the common home — the `HARNESS_PROGRAM` binding stays suite-local, being this suite's fixture rather than scaffolding.

`wire_memberless_harness` (61-71): a verbatim copy of emit.rs's builder — tmpdir, `.temper`, write `harness.ts`, `vendor_sdk` — whose own doc (58-60) cites `tests/emit.rs`'s `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it follows. Call the common builder with `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM`.
`wire_waypoint_harness` (117-123), the third copy (e76ec85), carrying the same doc cite back to emit.rs's builder. Call the common builder with `WAYPOINT_PROGRAM`.

acceptance: One builder composes tmpdir → `.temper` → `harness.ts` → `vendor_sdk`, in `tests/common`, and no suite re-derives it (engineering.md, "One job, one home": shared fixtures and builders live in one home, never copy-pasted per file). Behavior is unchanged, so the three suites' existing tests are the coverage; `cargo test` green.
notes: Found sweeping 74f4e62..ca4e866: e76ec85 added the third copy, its doc citing emit.rs's `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it followed rather than the home it should have extended — the copy-per-suite `tests/common`'s own header says it exists to end. Serialized behind the chain: shares projection_path_seam.rs with NESTED-FILE-LOCUS, builtin_lock_frozen.rs with SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS. scoped at 688d8cc.

summary: The node-set and edge-scope predicates move onto the selection algebra: count/unique/membership/degree/kind judge over any declared selection, not only a requirement's satisfier set

edit.description: The five predicates INDETERMINATE-NEVER-SILENT fenced out of the per-artifact facet (the arm at 492-509 and its comment block) gain the judge the fence stands in for: `contract.md`, "selection" states one algebra over selections with the quantifier as the clause's grain — no separate universal/existential machinery — so `Count`/`Unique`/`Membership` are whole-grain over any selection and `Degree`/`Kind` are each-grain over it. The fence entries retire as each gains its judge; `inadmissibilities` keeps only genuinely vacuous spellings (an empty `kind`, an inverted bound). `kind_violation` (516-527) is the existing each-grain entry point, pulled out because a satisfier's actual kind is roster context — the generalization is what lets it rejoin `decide`, or states why it cannot.

`roster` judges these five today over a requirement's satisfier set — the *only* selection they reach. The opt-in selection (`contract.md`, "selection": the members whose satisfies edge targets a requirement) is one selector among three, so this judging is the algebra's existential instance, never its home. Route it through the generalized judge rather than leaving a second implementation beside it (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home").

The reference-graph context `Degree` needs — the half `engine::decide` says "isn't even in scope here" (495-497). A by-kind or by-incidence selection needs the same context a requirement's satisfier scan has, so the graph reaches the judge for every selection, not one selector.

The clause's selection binding: the five carry their selection today only implicitly, via the requirement facet that hosts them. The `Predicate` enum (the closed vocabulary) does not grow — adding one is a deliberate language change and this is not one; what moves is which selections a clause may bind to.

The author-facing spelling of the five (`count`/`unique`/`membership`/`degree`/`kind`) — sayable outside a requirement's `clauses` array once the engine judges them there, so the SDK type surface stops implying the requirement facet is their only home.
The existing requirement-facet coverage: the five keep judging a requirement's satisfier set identically — the generalization adds selections, never moves the one that works.

tests.asserts: count/unique/membership are whole-grain over a by-kind selection, and degree/kind each-grain over it — the same verdicts the requirement facet reaches over its opt-in selection
a requirement's satisfier-set clauses are unchanged — the opt-in selection is one selector, not a second machinery

acceptance: count/unique/membership/degree/kind judge over any declared selection — by kind, by opt-in, by incidence — with the quantifier the clause's grain and no separate universal/existential machinery (contract.md, "selection"), and no per-artifact clause among them is inadmissible for want of a judge. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 inbox ruling, entry 2. No decision owed: the vocabulary does not grow — code lags explicit spec text (`contract.md`, "selection"), which the ruling states. The recut `engine.rs:492-504`'s own comments name it (REQUIREMENT-CLAUSES-RECUT). Serialized behind UNFILLED-EDGE-FIELD-NO-EDGE (shared src/main.rs wiring and the contract-clause test surface); file-disjoint from the NESTED-FILE chain, so the two run in parallel after it. scoped at d572827.

summary: `supporting-doc`'s reach clause: a supporting file is invisible unless its host skill references it — the by-kind universal binding, each-grain incidence, advisory

edit.description: `supporting-doc`'s default contract — the empty exported clause array SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS ships — gains its one clause: an incoming-degree bound over the kind's members, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16), the vendor fact that every supporting file is invisible unless SKILL.md references it. **Universal, not existential**: the fact holds of every supporting document, so the spelling is the by-kind binding with each-grain incidence (`contract.md`, "selection"), never a requirement — a requirement is the opt-in selector and would make a vendor fact consumer ceremony. **Reach is locus-agnostic**: any resolved edge from the host skill counts, post-0025's one edge noun and one degree semantics — a mention included, this clause being the "a contract may" that mention's obligation-free default reserves (`contract.md`, "edge"). Severity **advisory**, per `intent.md` invariant 5: a shipped coverage clause enters advisory, and escalation is the corpus's declared act.
The embedded built-in lock re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains its one clause row.
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the grown clause row.
`supporting-doc`'s default contract carries exactly one clause, at advisory severity, cited and guided — the shipped-clause bar (`builtins.md`, "The clauses live in code").

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains one clause row (the incoming-degree reach bound, advisory).

tests.asserts: `supporting-doc`'s default contract is one advisory reach clause, cited to the vendor fact and carrying its guidance
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically with the clause row

acceptance: A supporting document no edge from its host skill reaches is an advisory finding naming the vendor fact (builtins.md, "Default contracts"; the clause is decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated) — and reach counts any resolved edge, locus-agnostic. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 ruling's clause itself (25e9317 resolved the fork). The single-tag gate names PREDICATE-SELECTION-ALGEBRA — without it an incidence clause outside a requirement is inadmissible, so the clause cannot ship. It ALSO requires SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS: that entry mints the kind and its empty contract, and shares builtins.ts / builtin_lock.toml / builtin_lock_frozen.rs — so this is the queue tail, behind both chains. scoped at d572827.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at line 7) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at ca4e866: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml:7-9 still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
